message:
chore(beads): file 2 reality-check findings — fuzz-redact-secrets + readme-subcommand-gap

Reality-check sweep of README.md vs actual CLI found:

- All user-facing commands listed in README are implemented (no stubs /
  missing features). Zero substantive vision gaps.
- cass bakeoff and cass cast subcommands exist in the binary but are
  missing from README — filed as gp44p (P3 docs).
- src/indexer/redact_secrets.rs (production redaction path) has no
  libfuzzer harness despite running on every indexed message — filed
  as 4hveo (P2 fuzz). Existing fuzz targets cover chunked / config /
  decrypt / cli_argv / connectors / html_export / query_parser / kdf
  but not redact_secrets, which has a more complex surface (memoized
  JSON walker + 20+ regex patterns) that would benefit from
  structure-aware fuzzing.

No commit touches src/ — findings are filed as beads only so other
panes can pick them up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
